Operations command

Read pressure before you intervene.

Start with open work, launch timing, staffing gaps, and admin failures. Then move into the queue that still needs follow-up.

Role
Context
Mode
Use this lane for
Operational picture
System-wide read
Use this as the compressed top-of-fold picture for pipeline motion, delivery pressure, and broad failure signals before you touch a specific lane.
Decision ruleStay here when you need pattern recognition. Leave this pane only after you know whether the problem is workload, readiness, staffing, launch timing, or platform reliability.
Follow-up queue
Escalation and ownership control
Work from blocked tasks, overdue dispatch, and assignment drift first. This lane is for exceptions, not normal throughput.
Operating cueStay in this queue only long enough to reassign, unblock, or decide the next owner. If a task needs normal execution, push it back into the dedicated workspace instead of managing it from here.
Task Due / queue Owner Status Action
Legacy dispatch create
This form creates in the old dispatch system. Use unified Tasks center instead.
Roster pressure
Readiness and deployable capacity
Use this strip to see who is ready, who is restricted, and whether the current roster can absorb the next wave before you open the deeper People workspace.

Platform controls
